host_coupling.summary: The orchestration layer (commands/ppt.md) is deeply coupled to Claude Code-specific APIs. 15 occurrences of Task()/AskUserQuestion/SendMessage in ppt.md alone.

evidence.observation: Uses Task(subagent_type='ppt-agent:*') for all 4 agent types across 7 phases. Uses AskUserQuestion for 3 hard stops (Phase 2 research, Phase 4 outline, Phase 7 delivery). Uses SendMessage protocol (heartbeat, ready, error) for agent coordination.
All 4 agent definitions use Claude Code agent frontmatter (name, tools, model, color, effort, maxTurns, disallowedTools, memory). This is Claude Code's agent contract format — not portable.
Uses 'model: sonnet', 'effort: medium' — these are Claude-ecosystem knobs. OpenCode likely ignores them or maps differently.
evidence.detail: research-core and review-core use 'model: sonnet'; slide-core and content-core use 'model: opus'. This model routing is host-specific.

evidence.observation: Skill() invocation syntax is Claude Code plugin protocol. Other hosts would need their own invocation mechanism.
evidence.detail: review-core calls Skill(skill='ppt-agent:gemini-cli', args='...') — this is Claude Code's skill dispatch.

host_coupling.decoupling_assessment: The README mentions '核心解耦' as a long-term goal. Currently, the 7-phase workflow logic is entirely embedded in commands/ppt.md as prose instructions for the LLM. There is no separate core/ layer. Decoupling would require extracting the phase state machine, artifact contracts, and quality gates into a host-agnostic module — the workflow logic itself is sound, but it's expressed in LLM-prompt format rather than executable code.
host_coupling.recommendation: Extract phase state machine into a core/ module with: (1) phase transition logic, (2) artifact validation, (3) quality gate checks. Each host provides an adapter that maps Task→spawn, AskUserQuestion→prompt, SendMessage→signal.

mcp_server_migration.summary: MCP Server migration is feasible but non-trivial. The workflow has clear tool boundaries that map to MCP tools, but the 3 hard stops and agent coordination model need rethinking.

evidence.observation: Mentions 'MCP Server 化' with specific tool names: ppt/generate, ppt/outline, ppt/review. This shows the direction is already envisioned.
evidence.quote: 将核心工作流封装为 MCP Server，暴露 ppt/generate、ppt/outline、ppt/review 等标准 tools

evidence.observation: The workflow already uses file-based state (artifacts in RUN_DIR) as the durable truth layer. This is MCP-friendly — MCP tools can read/write the same artifact structure.

clean_mappings.notes: Parse args, create RUN_DIR, write scaffold. Stateless, pure function.
Takes topic + run_dir, writes isolated file. Embarrassingly parallel.
Reads outline.json, writes draft SVGs. Pure transformation.
Per-slide design. Needs style tokens and outline context.
Per-slide review. Gemini fallback logic stays internal.
File copy + HTML template population. Pure function.

hard_mappings.challenge: AskUserQuestion requires host-level user interaction. MCP tools are request-response, not interactive. Would need to split into ppt/research (returns findings) and ppt/confirm-requirements (takes user answers).
Same interaction pattern. Split into ppt/outline (returns preview) and ppt/approve-outline (takes feedback).
The draft_slide_ready signaling and sliding window parallelism require orchestration state that MCP tools don't naturally express. The host LLM would need to drive the pipeline.
hard_mappings.note: Could expose ppt/status tool that returns slide-status.json for the host to poll.

mcp_server_migration.recommendation: Split interactive phases into query+confirm pairs. Use ppt/status as a state query tool. The host LLM becomes the orchestrator (replacing commands/ppt.md), calling MCP tools in sequence. This trades prompt-driven orchestration for tool-driven orchestration.

headless_mode.summary: 3 hard stops block headless/CI execution. Template profiles could provide default answers, but outline approval is the hardest to automate.

evidence.observation: Phase 2 (research), Phase 4 (outline), and Phase 7 (delivery) are marked MANDATORY hard stops with 'Do NOT proceed until user responds/approves'.

hard_stops.purpose: User confirms target audience, purpose, key messages, tone, constraints
User approves/adjusts outline structure, page order, sections
User receives delivery summary and output files

phase_2.approach: Template profile with pre-set audience, purpose, tone, key messages
phase_2.example: --profile=investor-pitch.yaml would supply: audience=investors, purpose=persuade, tone=data-driven, key_messages=[revenue, growth, market]
phase_2.risk: low — profile just replaces user input, workflow continues normally

phase_4.approach: Auto-approve first outline if it passes structural validation (page count in range, all required sections present, MECE check)
phase_4.risk: medium — outline quality is subjective. Auto-approval may produce misaligned structure. Could add --auto-approve-outline flag with structural validator.

phase_7.approach: No interaction needed in headless — just write output files and exit with status code
phase_7.risk: none — delivery is already a pure output step

headless_mode.recommendation: Phase 7 is trivially headless. Phase 2 needs template profiles. Phase 4 is the bottleneck — offer --outline for pre-built outlines or --auto-approve-outline with structural validation. The --headless flag should combine these defaults.

style_extensibility.summary: Style system is well-designed with a registry (index.json) and YAML token files. Adding a new style requires 2 steps but lacks documentation for end users.

evidence.observation: 4 styles registered: business, tech, creative, minimal. Each has id, domain, file_path, keywords, description, use_cases. Style discovery is registry-driven (filter domain=='style'), not hardcoded.
Style YAML is well-structured with 7 sections: color_scheme, typography, card_style, gradients, elevation, decoration, slide_type_overrides. Extended v1.1 format.

Phase 1 explicitly states: 'Available styles are discovered from skills/_shared/index.json (filter resources where domain == style). Do NOT hardcode style names — read the registry.' This is good extensibility practice.
Brand color override documented with --brand-colors flag and YAML format example. But no documentation on how to add a completely new style.

add_style_workflow.step_1: Create a new YAML file in skills/_shared/references/styles/<name>.yaml following the v1.1 schema (color_scheme, typography, card_style, gradients, elevation, decoration, slide_type_overrides)
add_style_workflow.step_2: Add a new entry in skills/_shared/index.json with domain='style', unique id, file_path, keywords, description, use_cases

style_extensibility.schema_validation: No schema validation for style YAML files. A malformed YAML (missing required fields like color_scheme.primary) would cause silent failures in slide-core. No JSON Schema for index.json entries either.
style_extensibility.recommendation: Document the add-style workflow in README or a contributing guide. Consider adding a style YAML schema validator (could be a simple script that checks required fields). The 2-file workflow is clean but undiscoverable without documentation.

gemini_dependency.summary: Gemini fallback is well-designed in documentation but has an untested edge: the gemini CLI binary missing entirely (not just model unavailability).

evidence.observation: Script tries models in order: default → gemini-2.5-pro → gemini-2.5-flash. On all failures, exits with code 2. On spawn error (gemini binary not found), also returns success=false. So missing binary → all models fail → exit 2 → Claude fallback. The path IS covered.
evidence.binary_missing_handling: spawnSync error caught at line 91-93, returns success=false

evidence.observation: Fallback strategy explicitly documented: exit 2 → Claude self-optimization using same quality standards. 'optimization must still happen, just without the cross-model perspective.'
review-core handles exit codes: 0=Gemini available, 2=Gemini unavailable (Claude fallback), 1=script error (fix and retry). The protocol is clear.

untested_edges: npx/tsx not available — invoke-gemini-ppt.ts won't run at all. This is a hard dependency on Node.js ecosystem.
No test suite exists to verify the fallback path works end-to-end.
If Gemini returns malformed output (exit 0 but unparseable response), the current code treats non-empty stdout as success. review-core must handle parse failures.
The Claude self-review fallback has never been benchmarked against Gemini review for quality parity.

node_dependency.fallback_if_missing: None documented. If Node.js is missing, the entire gemini-cli skill fails. review-core would need to detect this and go straight to Claude self-review.
node_dependency.recommendation: Add a check in review-core: if npx/tsx is not available, skip gemini-cli invocation entirely and go to Claude self-review. Or rewrite invoke-gemini-ppt.ts as a shell script to remove the Node.js dependency.

gemini_dependency.recommendation: The Gemini fallback design is solid. Key gaps: (1) Node.js hard dependency for the wrapper script, (2) no end-to-end test for fallback path, (3) no parse-failure handling for malformed Gemini output.

output_format.summary: SVG-only output. PPTX/PDF export would require external tools and significant work but is architecturally feasible.

evidence.observation: All slides are 1280x720 SVG files. The workflow is SVG-native throughout: draft SVGs → design SVGs → output SVGs + HTML preview.
SVG spec mandates: no external dependencies, all graphics inline, no <image xlink:href> to external files. This is clean but means no raster image support.

pptx.approach: Use python-pptx or a Node.js library to convert SVG slides to PPTX. Each SVG becomes a slide background or embedded image.
challenges: SVG text elements would become rasterized images in PPTX, losing editability
python-pptx cannot natively render SVG — would need SVG→PNG conversion first (e.g., via Playwright/Puppeteer)
Font rendering differences between SVG and PPTX
Bento Grid cards cannot map 1:1 to PPTX shapes without a layout parser
pptx.quality_loss: medium — text becomes non-editable, layout is pixel-perfect but frozen

pdf.approach: Use Puppeteer/Playwright to render SVGs to PDF pages, or use rsvg-convert/Inkscape CLI
challenges: CJK font availability on headless systems
Multi-page PDF assembly from individual SVG files
pdf.quality_loss: minimal — SVG→PDF is a well-solved rendering problem

png.approach: Already partially solved — docs/images/ contains PNG exports of SVG slides (visible in README). Likely manual conversion.
png.tool: Puppeteer screenshot, rsvg-convert, or Inkscape CLI

output_format.recommendation: PDF export is the lowest-effort win (SVG→PDF via rsvg-convert or Puppeteer). PPTX with editable text would require a fundamentally different output pipeline. PNG export could be added as a Phase 7 option.

plugin_distribution.summary: Plugin manifest and marketplace config are minimal. Missing versioning strategy, dependency declaration, compatibility metadata, and update mechanism.

evidence.observation: Only 3 fields: name, version, description. No dependencies, no compatibility range, no license, no author, no repository URL.
Minimal: name, owner, plugins array with name+description+source. No version history, no install count tracking, no category tags.
Install instructions: 'claude plugin marketplace add zengwenliang416/ppt-agent' then 'claude plugin install ppt-agent'. No version pinning, no update command documented.

versioning_gaps: plugin.json has version 1.0.0 but CHANGELOG.md shows [Unreleased] — version is static, not bumped with changes
No version history or release tagging in marketplace.json
No semver strategy documented — when would version bump to 1.1.0 vs 2.0.0?
No mechanism for users to check for updates or pin to a specific version
dependency_gaps: Runtime dependency on Node.js (for gemini-cli) not declared anywhere in plugin metadata
Optional dependency on gemini CLI not declared
Optional dependency on curl, gh, yt-dlp, xreach, mcporter not declared (these are gracefully degraded, but should be documented in metadata)
No min/max Claude Code version compatibility declared
plugin_distribution.recommendation: Enrich plugin.json with: dependencies (node>=18), optionalDependencies (gemini), engine compatibility, license, author. Add release workflow that bumps version + tags. Document update mechanism in README.

testing.summary: Zero test files in the entire project. No unit tests, no integration tests, no snapshot tests. Quality regressions would be caught only by manual end-to-end runs.

evidence.observation: Glob for **/*.test.* and **/*.spec.* returned empty. No test directory. No test framework configured (no jest/vitest/mocha in package.json — no package.json at all).

The only executable code is invoke-gemini-ppt.ts (167 lines). Everything else is LLM prompts (markdown) and configuration (YAML/JSON). Traditional unit testing doesn't apply well to prompt-driven workflows.

testable_components.what_to_test: arg parsing, role prompt loading, fallback model chain, exit code handling, output file writing
testable_components.value: medium — only executable code, but well-tested by real usage

testable_components.what_to_test: JSON output validity, tool detection accuracy, graceful handling when tools are missing
All 4 style YAMLs conform to expected schema (required fields present, valid hex colors, numeric values in range)
testable_components.value: high — prevents silent failures from malformed styles

testable_components.what_to_test: All referenced file_path values exist, no duplicate ids, required fields present
Generated outlines conform to schema defined in outline-architect.md
testable_components.value: high — outline.json is the central contract

testable_components.what_to_test: Generated SVGs have valid XML, correct viewBox, font-size floor, safe area margins — the same pre-review checks that review-core does
testable_components.value: high — catches regressions in slide-core output quality

testable_components.what_to_test: Placeholder replacement produces valid HTML, SVG embedding works, keyboard navigation functions
Full workflow with mock LLM responses produces expected artifact structure in RUN_DIR
testable_components.value: high — but requires LLM mocking infrastructure

testing.recommendation: Start with low-effort, high-value tests: (1) Style YAML schema validator, (2) index.json registry validator, (3) invoke-gemini-ppt.ts unit tests, (4) probe.sh output validation. These are all executable without LLM calls. For LLM-driven phases, focus on artifact contract validation (does outline.json match schema? does SVG pass structural checks?).

cross_platform_verified.summary: README claims 4 verified platform×model combinations. The workflow is model-agnostic (prompts work with any capable LLM) but host-specific (Claude Code plugin protocol).

evidence.observation: Verified: GPT-5.4+OpenCode, MiniMax M2.5+OpenCode, MiMo V2 Pro+OpenCode, Claude Opus+Claude Code. 3 of 4 are on OpenCode, suggesting OpenCode compatibility is the primary cross-platform axis.

claude_code.notes: Plugin protocol, agent frontmatter, Task/AskUserQuestion/SendMessage all native

opencode.notes: OpenCode must support the same or equivalent plugin protocol. The README shows it works but doesn't document what adaptations were needed. Does OpenCode support Task()? Skill()? agent frontmatter? Or does it use a different mechanism?
opencode.question: How does OpenCode consume this plugin? Does it read commands/ppt.md directly? Does it support the same agent spawning?

cursor.notes: Mentioned in README roadmap as potential MCP target. Currently no adapter.

codex.notes: Mentioned in README roadmap. Would need MCP Server or Codex-specific adapter.

cross_platform_verified.recommendation: Document what adaptations OpenCode uses. If OpenCode has its own Task/Agent protocol, document the mapping. This is crucial knowledge for the '核心解耦' goal.

cross_cutting_observations.observation: The entire workflow logic lives in markdown files (commands/ppt.md, agents/*.md, skills/*/SKILL.md). The only executable code is invoke-gemini-ppt.ts (167 lines) and probe.sh (42 lines). This makes the project highly portable to any LLM that can follow structured instructions, but hard to test with traditional methods.
cross_cutting_observations.implication: Testing and validation must focus on artifact contracts (JSON schemas, SVG structural rules) rather than code coverage. The 'code' is the prompts.

cross_cutting_observations.observation: The workflow state is entirely file-based (artifact presence in RUN_DIR determines resume point). This is elegant and host-agnostic — any system that can read/write files can participate. But there's no lock file or concurrency guard.
cross_cutting_observations.implication: Concurrent runs to the same RUN_DIR could corrupt state. Not a problem in current single-user mode but would matter for CI/headless.

cross_cutting_observations.observation: Despite having a TypeScript file (invoke-gemini-ppt.ts), there's no package.json. The script uses 'npx tsx' to run directly. This means no dependency management, no version locking, no test script entry point.
cross_cutting_observations.implication: Adding a package.json with tsx as a devDependency would improve reproducibility and enable a test script.

cross_cutting_observations.observation: agent-reach has a comprehensive degradation strategy (Tier 2 → Tier 1 → Tier 0 → WebSearch). gemini-cli has a model fallback chain + Claude self-review fallback. This pattern is consistent and well-documented.
cross_cutting_observations.implication: This is a strength. The same principle should be applied to the Node.js dependency (fallback if npx/tsx missing) and to the host coupling (graceful degradation across hosts).

optimization_opportunities.title: Extract core/ workflow engine
optimization_opportunities.description: Extract the 7-phase state machine from commands/ppt.md into a host-agnostic core/ module. Define adapter interfaces for spawn_agent, ask_user, send_signal. This is the prerequisite for MCP Server migration and true cross-platform support.
optimization_opportunities.blocked_by: Requires design decision: core/ as executable code (TypeScript/Python) or as a structured specification (JSON state machine)?

optimization_opportunities.title: Add headless mode with template profiles
optimization_opportunities.description: Add --headless flag, --profile for Phase 2 defaults, --outline for Phase 4 bypass, --auto-approve-outline for structural auto-approval. Phase 7 is already headless-compatible.
optimization_opportunities.blocked_by: Nothing — can be added incrementally to commands/ppt.md

optimization_opportunities.title: Add artifact schema validators
optimization_opportunities.description: Create validation scripts for: style YAML schema, index.json registry integrity, outline.json schema, SVG structural rules (viewBox, font-size, safe area). These serve as both tests and runtime guards.

optimization_opportunities.title: Enrich plugin metadata
optimization_opportunities.description: Add dependencies, compatibility, license, author, keywords to plugin.json. Add version bumping workflow. Document update mechanism.

optimization_opportunities.title: Add PDF export option
optimization_opportunities.description: Add --format=pdf flag to Phase 7. Use rsvg-convert or Puppeteer to render SVG→PDF. Gracefully degrade if tool is missing.

optimization_opportunities.title: Document style authoring workflow
optimization_opportunities.description: Add a section in README or a dedicated guide explaining how to create a new style: YAML schema, index.json registration, token reference.

optimization_opportunities.title: Add Node.js fallback for gemini-cli
optimization_opportunities.description: In review-core, detect if npx/tsx is available before invoking gemini-cli. If missing, go directly to Claude self-review. Or rewrite invoke-gemini-ppt.ts as a POSIX shell script to eliminate the Node.js dependency entirely.

optimization_opportunities.title: Document OpenCode adaptation mechanism
optimization_opportunities.description: Document how OpenCode consumes this plugin — what protocol mapping exists, what adaptations were needed. Critical for the cross-platform roadmap.
optimization_opportunities.blocked_by: Requires knowledge of OpenCode's plugin protocol
